requirement.original: 阅读我最近的allow和do开发的dev文档，告诉我我不是已经彻底允许allow允许任何指令了吗？为什么仍然出现：The /allow bypass cleared the orchestrator gate but pretool-git-privilege-guard.py is a separate hook that independently blocks git commit from any agent/Claude context. It can't be bypassed from inside the session.
requirement.clarified: Add _check_git_allowlist() call to _evaluate_commit() in pretool-git-privilege-guard.py so that the main agent can bypass git commit via /allow, consistent with how /allow already bypasses git merge in the same file.
requirement.what: Add one _check_git_allowlist(command, data) call inside _evaluate_commit() in hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py, and update the block error message to reflect that /allow now works for main agent
requirement.why: The user fixed /allow to bypass pretool-bash-safety.sh (commit c6653c0), but pretool-git-privilege-guard.py is a second independent PreToolUse hook. _evaluate_commit() never calls _check_git_allowlist(), making git commit unbypassable via /allow even though every other evaluate-* function in the same file already has this call.

excluded: _evaluate_push() — intentionally uses Scheme 6 grant mechanism, not allowlist
_check_git_allowlist() internals — already correct, returns False for subagents

success_criteria: Main agent can run git commit after /allow with a matching pattern, without being blocked by pretool-git-privilege-guard.py
Subagents are still blocked (no change to subagent behavior)
Error message no longer claims git commit cannot be bypassed from inside the session
constraints: _check_git_allowlist() call must come AFTER BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE check (preserve fast-path for auto-bulk commits)
_check_git_allowlist() is read-only — PostToolUse handles grant deletion; no double-consume risk
Subagent exclusion is inside _check_git_allowlist() itself — no call-site guard needed

out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py (handles grant deletion; no change needed)

root_cause_analysis.symptom: After /allow, git commit is still blocked by pretool-git-privilege-guard.py even though the orchestrator gate and pretool-bash-safety.sh are both cleared.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: _evaluate_commit() in pretool-git-privilege-guard.py does not call _check_git_allowlist(command, data). Every other evaluate-* function in the same file (_evaluate_merge, _evaluate_reset_hard, _evaluate_direct_ref_mutation, _evaluate_forbidden_plumbing) calls it; _evaluate_commit is the sole exception.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: c6653c0 — this commit fixed pretool-bash-safety.sh but did not touch pretool-git-privilege-guard.py
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The privilege guard was built before /allow was a true break-glass. When /allow bypass was added to evaluate-* functions (merge, reset-hard, etc.), _evaluate_commit was intentionally kept tighter because git commit has higher destructive potential. The previous '永久彻底修复' cycle only updated pretool-bash-safety.sh, not the guard.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: The user expects /allow to mean 'bypass any command I explicitly grant'. It does so for pretool-bash-safety.sh but not for the privilege guard's commit path. This is an internal consistency gap: git merge is /allow-bypassable but git commit is not, within the same hook file.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Gap existed since the privilege guard was introduced; /allow was later made a true break-glass in pretool-bash-safety.sh (c6653c0, 2026-05-09) but the guard was not updated simultaneously.
observations: Read pretool-git-privilege-guard.py in full (693 lines) — confirmed _evaluate_commit() lines 449-471 contain zero calls to _check_git_allowlist()
_evaluate_merge() line 477: if _check_git_allowlist(command, data): return — present (inferred from Read)
_evaluate_reset_hard() line 596: if _check_git_allowlist(command, data): return — present (inferred from Read)
_evaluate_direct_ref_mutation() line 609: if _check_git_allowlist(command, data): return — present (inferred from Read)
_evaluate_forbidden_plumbing() line 642: if _check_git_allowlist(command, data): return — present (inferred from Read)
_evaluate_commit() has no _check_git_allowlist call anywhere — confirmed absent (inferred from Read)
pretool-bash-safety.sh line 615: check_and_consume_allowlist runs as global short-circuit BEFORE all blocks — confirmed (inferred from Read, cross-referenced with commit c6653c0 diff)

Live evidence: running codex exec with the word 'commit' in the prompt triggered pretool-git-privilege-guard.py blocking (observed during this BA session — confirms the guard matches commit anywhere in the Bash command, not just as a git verb)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred from source; no Playwright/runtime observation possible for hook execution path)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: User quoted verbatim error message containing 'pretool-git-privilege-guard.py is a separate hook'. Error message text in _block_default_deny_commit() was grep-matched to the file at lines 433-446. Confirmed by reading the full function.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: N/A — the gap is a missing feature (no-call-added), not a regression introduced by a specific commit

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: 我不是已经彻底允许allow允许任何指令了吗
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: After fix: main agent can run git commit following /allow with a matching pattern

component_chain.role: main() entry point — loads stdin JSON, checks /do consent (line 682), then calls _evaluate_command()
_evaluate_command() — dispatches to individual evaluate-* functions based on git verb detection
_evaluate_commit() — the function with the gap; checks BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE and grant files but NOT _check_git_allowlist
_check_git_allowlist() — reads /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-<sid>.json, returns True on match (read-only; main-agent only due to agent_id check at line 146)
component_chain.evidence: Read lines 141-177 — line 146: if data.get('agent_id'): return False

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Prevents subagents from using the /allow bypass — subagents cannot call _check_git_allowlist successfully
Fast-path allow for auto-bulk blessed-bridge commits from /merge; must remain as first check in _evaluate_commit()
Prevents subagents from using /do consent bypass in _has_do_consent()

setup.reason: non-UI -- hook; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: The /allow bypass cleared the orchestrator gate but pretool-git-privilege-guard.py is a separate hook that independently blocks git commit from any agent/Claude context. It can't be bypassed from inside the session.

measured.value: _evaluate_commit() contains zero calls to _check_git_allowlist(command, data); _evaluate_merge() at line 477 has 'if _check_git_allowlist(command, data): return'

expected.value: _evaluate_commit() calls _check_git_allowlist(command, data) after the BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE check, identical to _evaluate_merge()

evidence.gap: _evaluate_commit() is missing one _check_git_allowlist(command, data) call; _evaluate_merge() has it; these two functions should be symmetric with respect to /allow bypass

reference_source.description: _evaluate_merge() in hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py — the canonical pattern for adding /allow bypass to an evaluate-* function in this file

attempts.artifact_path: commit c6653c0 (fix(hooks): /allow + /do behavior + english-only durable prevention)
attempts.proposed_solution: Relocate global /allow short-circuit in pretool-bash-safety.sh to run before all Layer 1.x blocks
attempts.actual_change: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh line 615 — check_and_consume_allowlist now runs before daemon-restart prohibition and all other blocks
attempts.outcome: pretool-bash-safety.sh now fully bypassed by /allow, but pretool-git-privilege-guard.py is independent and was not modified. git commit still blocked.

novelty_check.rationale: Prior attempt modified pretool-bash-safety.sh. This attempt modifies pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:_evaluate_commit() — different file, different function, same layer. The prior attempt correctly fixed the bash safety hook; this attempt completes the fix by updating the privilege guard's commit evaluation path.

context.codebase_state: hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py at HEAD (refactor aaf3c44 made _check_git_allowlist read-only — no unlink inside the function, delegation to PostToolUse). Two independent PreToolUse hooks fire for every Bash call: pretool-bash-safety.sh and pretool-git-privilege-guard.py. Both must pass (exit 0) for the tool call to proceed.
context.recent_commits: c6653c0 fix(hooks): /allow + /do behavior; aaf3c44 refactor(hooks): extract shared lib, fix git-privilege-guard read-only

development_approach.strategy: Add the missing _check_git_allowlist(command, data) call to _evaluate_commit() after the BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE check. Update the error message in _block_default_deny_commit() to remove the false 'can't be bypassed' claim.
development_approach.validation_approach: Read the modified file and confirm: (1) _evaluate_commit() calls _check_git_allowlist after line 453 and before line 457; (2) _block_default_deny_commit() no longer contains 'can't be bypassed from inside the session'; (3) no other functions are modified

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex exec invocation was blocked by pretool-git-privilege-guard.py itself — the word 'commit' in the prompt text triggered the guard's _looks_like_git_commit() regex on the full Bash command string. This is live observational evidence that the guard matches 'commit' anywhere in the command, not just as a git verb. Three invocation attempts all failed. Self-review substituted.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Self-review performed covering: (1) grant double-consumption risk — confirmed safe because _check_git_allowlist is read-only (refactor aaf3c44); (2) insertion order — BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE check must remain first; (3) subagent exclusion — handled inside _check_git_allowlist itself; (4) push path exemption — _evaluate_push intentionally uses Scheme 6 grants, not allowlist; (5) error message update scope — narrow to _block_default_deny_commit() only. No scope expansion identified.
